children.label: A
children.text: is more than the amount of insurance or demonstration of financial responsibility required under ; andsection 50914(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: is not more than $1,500,000,000 (plus additional amounts necessary to reflect inflation occurring after ) above that insurance or financial responsibility amount.January 1, 1989

The Secretary may not provide for paying a part of a claim for which death, bodily injury, or property damage or loss results from willful misconduct by the licensee or transferee. To the extent insurance required under is not available to cover a successful third party liability claim because of an insurance policy exclusion the Secretary decides is usual for the type of insurance involved, the Secretary may provide for paying the excluded claims without regard to the limitation contained in section 50914(a)(1).section 50914(a)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a licensee or transferee under this chapter;

children.label: ii
children.text: a contractor, subcontractor, or customer of the licensee or transferee;

children.label: iii
children.text: a contractor or subcontractor of a customer; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a space flight participant.

children.label: B
children.text: Clause (iv) of subparagraph (A) ceases to be effective .September 30, 2028

notice must be given to the Government of a claim, or a civil action related to the claim, against a party described in subsection (a)(1) of this section for death, bodily injury, or property damage or loss;
the Government must be given an opportunity to participate or assist in the defense of the claim or action; and
the Secretary must approve any part of a settlement to be paid out of appropriations of the Government.

children.heading: .—Withholding Payments
children.label: c .—Withholding Payments
children.text: The Secretary may withhold a payment under subsection (a) of this section if the Secretary certifies that the amount is not reasonable. However, the Secretary shall deem to be reasonable the amount of a claim finally decided by a court of competent jurisdiction.

children.label: A
children.text: survey the causes and extent of damage; and

children.label: B
children.text: submit expeditiously to Congress a report on the results of the survey.

children.label: A
children.text: outlines the total dollar value of the claims;

children.label: B
children.text: recommends sources of amounts to pay for the claims;

children.label: C
children.text: includes legislative language required to carry out the plan if additional legislative authority is required; and

children.label: D
children.text: for a single event or incident, may not be for more than $1,500,000,000.

children.label: A
children.text: have an identification number; and

children.label: B
children.text: be submitted to the Senate and the House of Representatives on the same day and when the Senate and House are in session.

children.label: A
children.text: means a joint resolution of Congress the matter after the resolving clause of which is as follows: “That the Congress approves the compensation plan numbered _____ submitted to the Congress on _____ __, 20__.”, with the blank spaces being filled appropriately; but

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a resolution that includes more than one compensation plan.

The Senate shall consider under this subsection a compensation plan requiring additional appropriations or legislative authority not later than 60 calendar days of continuous session of Congress after the date on which the plan is submitted to Congress.
A resolution introduced in the Senate shall be referred immediately to a committee by the President of the Senate. All resolutions related to the same plan shall be referred to the same committee.

children.label: A
children.text: If the committee of the Senate to which a resolution has been referred does not report the resolution within 20 calendar days after it is referred, a motion is in order to discharge the committee from further consideration of the resolution or to discharge the committee from further consideration of the plan.

children.label: B
children.text: A motion to discharge may be made only by an individual favoring the resolution and is highly privileged (except that the motion may not be made after the committee has reported a resolution on the plan). Debate on the motion is limited to one hour, to be divided equally between those favoring and those opposing the resolution. An amendment to the motion is not in order. A motion to reconsider the vote by which the motion is agreed to or disagreed to is not in order.

children.label: C
children.text: If the motion to discharge is agreed to or disagreed to, the motion may not be renewed and another motion to discharge the committee from another resolution on the same plan may not be made.

children.label: A
children.text: After a committee of the Senate reports, or is discharged from further consideration of, a resolution, a motion to proceed to the consideration of the resolution is in order at any time, even though a similar previous motion has been disagreed to. The motion is highly privileged and is not debatable. An amendment to the motion is not in order. A motion to reconsider the vote by which the motion is agreed to or disagreed to is not in order.

children.label: B
children.text: Debate on the resolution referred to in subparagraph (A) of this paragraph is limited to not more than 10 hours, to be divided equally between those favoring and those opposing the resolution. A motion further to limit debate is not debatable. An amendment to, or motion to recommit, the resolution is not in order. A motion to reconsider the vote by which the resolution is agreed to or disagreed to is not in order.

children.label: A
children.text: a motion to postpone related to the discharge from committee.

children.label: B
children.text: a motion to postpone consideration of a resolution.

children.label: C
children.text: a motion to proceed to the consideration of other business.

children.label: D
children.text: an appeal from a decision of the chair related to the application of the rules of the Senate to the procedures related to a resolution.

children.heading: .—Application
children.label: f .—Application
children.text: This section applies to a license issued or transferred under this chapter for which the Secretary receives a complete and valid application not later than . This section does not apply to permits.September 30, 2028
